title: UI-138 — Foundation module nav route contracts + landing stubs
prompt:
# UI-138 — Foundation module nav route contracts + landing stubs

Wave: `app-shell-foundation-20260520` (00-foundation).
Project root: `/Users/mariuszkrawczyk/Projects/monopilot-kira`.

## Goal
Make the full AppSidebar safe to use: every active desktop nav item from UI-128 must resolve to a non-404 localized route under `(app)`, while `scanner` remains excluded from desktop nav and isolated under `(scanner)`.

This task does not implement module business screens. It creates minimal shell-compatible module landing stubs only where a module root does not already exist, plus a route-contract Playwright spec that will fail if any sidebar link 404s.

## Files (scope)
- `apps/web/app/[locale]/(app)/(modules)/<module>/page.tsx` [create only for missing roots; use the existing project route convention if T-133 selected a different transparent group]
- `apps/web/e2e/module-nav-route-contract.spec.ts` [create]
- `apps/web/components/shell/__tests__/module-nav-route-contract.test.tsx` [create]
- `apps/web/lib/navigation/app-nav.ts` and `module-registry.ts` [read/modify only if needed to export helpers; UI-128 owns the data]

Required active desktop routes from UI-128:
`/`, `/settings`, `/npd`, `/technical`, `/planning`, `/scheduler`, `/warehouse`, `/production`, `/quality`, `/finance`, `/shipping`, `/reporting`, `/maintenance`, `/multi-site`, `/oee`.

## Landing stub contract
If a route root is missing, create a Server Component page that:
- renders inside `(app)` shell, not standalone;
- uses `PageHeader` from UI-132 when available;
- displays translated module name and a short "Module shell ready; feature screens land in module wave" message;
- includes `data-testid="module-landing-<module_id>"`;
- has no fake business data, no fixture tables, no mocked KPIs.

Do not create a landing stub for `foundation` or `scanner`. Scanner stays at `/en/dev/scanner` in T-134 as an e2e harness only.

## Acceptance criteria
1. Every sidebar-exposed UI-128 route returns 200 or intentional auth redirect, never 404, for locale `/en`; route matrix includes Scheduler, Reporting, and Multi-Site.
2. Route stubs are created only for missing module roots; existing module pages are not overwritten.
3. Stub pages render inside `data-testid="app-shell"` and include exactly one `module-landing-<module_id>` marker; login/scanner routes do not render AppShell.
4. Scanner is not present in `APP_NAV_GROUPS` and `/en/dev/scanner` renders ScannerFrame without AppSidebar/AppTopbar.
5. Playwright `module-nav-route-contract.spec.ts` clicks every AppSidebar link and asserts URL + non-404 + active nav state.
6. `pnpm exec playwright test apps/web/e2e/module-nav-route-contract.spec.ts`, `pnpm --filter web test module-nav-route-contract -- --run`, typecheck, and lint pass.

## Test strategy
- RED: write module-nav-route-contract Playwright spec before adding missing stubs; it should fail on the first missing route.
- Create only the minimum missing Server Component stubs.
- Run the route contract spec, then the shell-smoke spec from T-136.
- Run `pnpm --filter web typecheck && pnpm --filter web lint`.

## Risk red lines
- Do not implement module business UI or fake dashboards in landing stubs.
- Do not overwrite existing route pages; compare first, then skip if present.
- Do not add scanner to AppSidebar.
- Do not place user-visible pages outside `apps/web/app/[locale]/...`.
- Do not modify `proxy.ts`.

## Closeout
Provide route matrix evidence for every sidebar link, Playwright screenshots for at least Dashboard/Reporting/Multi-Site/Scheduler, console_network_check, changed_files, test commands/results, and a list of any existing pages that were left untouched.

pnpm exec playwright test apps/web/e2e/module-nav-route-contract.spec.ts and pnpm --filter web test module-nav-route-contract -- --run pass; typecheck and lint pass
